_comment: repo-router emits a single JSON object on stdout with {slug, owner, repo, default_branch, fix_src_path}. The Python original is invoked from a scratch dir where a synthetic sfp.env + trp.env are staged; the TS port anchors REPO_ROOT to process.cwd() at module load, so the test chdirs into the same scratch dir and dynamic-imports the module. Both paths embed the scratch dir's absolute path in fix_src_path — the sanitize step replaces that runtime prefix with the placeholder token <REPO_ROOT> in the actual bytes so the compare against expected.txt is stable across operator machines. No other bytes are touched. Expected.txt was generated from a subprocess run of the .py original and re-serialized via Node JSON.stringify (no indent, no key sort) so the comparator matches the .ts port's exact stdout format — the .py's default `json.dump` uses `', '` / `': '` separators while Node's `JSON.stringify` uses `','` / `':'`; this fixture pins the Node canonical form because the shape-(a) contract is the JSON value, not the byte layout of one particular serializer.
sanitize_impl: Replace every occurrence of the runtime scratch REPO_ROOT (equal to the test's tempDir absolute path) with the literal string '<REPO_ROOT>' in the captured stdout bytes; then compare byte-for-byte against expected.txt.
shape_reason: primary output is a pure return value serialized as a single JSON line on stdout — the driver's callers read it via `JSON.parse(stdout)` and never treat it as free-form text
